Commercial Banking Officer Job at Abbey Mortgage Bank Plc




        
    

        
        Abbey Mortgage Bank Plc is the premier, largest and most profitable, non-aligned Primary Mortgage Bank in Nigeria. Abbey is one of the seven national Primary Mortgage Banks (PMB) licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria and Federal Mortgage Bank of Nigeria (FMBN). The recent recapitalization exercise saw the downsize from 105 mortgage banks to only 34 now surviving. We not only emerged successfully capitalized but one of the few that also met our National obligation as well as amongst the public liability companies. 

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Commercial Banking Officer

Job Locations: Abuja, Delta, and Lagos
Job type: Full-time
     

            
    



Job Description


	We are looking for experienced and target-driven Commercial Banking Officers to drive business growth, customer acquisition, deposit mobilization, and revenue generation.


Key Responsibilities


	Drive deposit mobilization and achieve assigned growth targets.
	Identify and acquire new customers While maintaining
	strong relationships With existing clients.
	Aggressively market the Bank's products and services to drive customer acquisition and revenue growth.
	Cross-sell and up-sell banking products to maximize profitability.
	Monitor Customer portfolios and ensure excellent service delivery.
	Prepare business performance reports and perform other duties as assigned.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in any relevant discipline.
	3-5 years of experience in Commercial Banking,  Business Development, Sales, or Relationship Management.
	Proven track record in deposit mobilization and customer acquisition.
	Strong communication, negotiation, and relationship management skills.
	Results-oriented with the ability to meet targets.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

HR Business Partner Job at Abbey Mortgage Bank Plc




        
    

        
        Abbey Mortgage Bank Plc is the premier, largest and most profitable, non-aligned Primary Mortgage Bank in Nigeria. Abbey is one of the seven national Primary Mortgage Banks (PMB) licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria and Federal Mortgage Bank of Nigeria (FMBN). The recent recapitalization exercise saw the downsize from 105 mortgage banks to only 34 now surviving. We not only emerged successfully capitalized but one of the few that also met our National obligation as well as amongst the public liability companies. 

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Title: HR Business Partner

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Define and implement People & Culture strategies aligned with business goals.
	Lead workforce planning, talent forecasting, and organizational design.
	Manage end-to-end employee lifecycle: recruitment, onboarding, development, engagement, and offboarding.
	Drive performance management and leadership development initiatives.
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian labor laws and internal policies.
	Support organizational change and cultural transformation programs.


Requirements


	Bachelor's degree in HR, Business Administration, or related field; advanced degree or HR certification preferred.
	5-8 years in strategic HR roles, ideally in financial services or banking.
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian labor law and HR best practices.
	Experience in change management, work- force planning, and performance management.
	Commercial banking experience is an added advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

  "title": "",
  "description": "Rivy (YC 21) is enabling clean energy transition in Nigeria. We provide seamless finance to end-users, installers, and operators of clean energy equipment through our proprietary credit engine. We are committed to building innovative solutions that transform the way the industry operates and focused on providing exceptional products and services to our customers.&nbsp;

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.)&nbsp;Senior Associate / Sales Associate

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time (Hybrid)
Department: Sales &amp; Growth
Reports To: Senior Manager Sales

The Role


	The successful candidate will manage the growth of the company&rsquo;s sales lending portfolio .
	The candidate will also maintain client relationships, create sales strategies, and drive new business development while working closely with other departments to ensure the sales team&#39;s success.


Job Responsibilities
?Sales Strategy and Planning:

Team Leadership and Development:


	Lead, motivate, and mentor the sales associates to achieve individual and team sales targets.
	Provide training, guidance, and support to sales representatives to improve skills and ensure they meet performance expectations.

	Foster a high-performance culture focused on accountability, collaboration, and results.


Client Relationship&nbsp;&nbsp;Management:


	Develop and maintain strong relationships with key clients and partners.
	Ensure customer satisfaction through proactive problem-solving, addressing concerns, and providing excellent service.
	Assist in negotiating and closing high-value sales or complex deals, ensuring favorable terms for the company.
	Collaborate with the customer service team to ensure smooth post-sale experiences and customer retention.


Sales Performance Monitoring:


	Monitor the performance of the sales team through regular reporting, analyzing sales activities, and tracking performance against goals.
	Identify areas of underperformance and develop corrective action plans to drive improvements.
	Use CRM tools to track sales activities, opportunities, and client interactions.


Market Research and Competitive Analysis:


	Conduct market research to stay informed of industry trends, competitor activities, and customer needs.
	Analyze customer feedback and competitor strategies to inform sales strategies and stay ahead of market demands.
	Adjust sales tactics as necessary based on market conditions and evolving business needs.


Collaboration with Cross-Functional Teams:


	Work closely with marketing to align sales campaigns, promotional activities, and lead-generation strategies.
	Collaborate with internal teams (marketing, credit, engineering, etc.) to ensure product offerings are aligned with customer needs and feedback.
	Provide insights from customers to help shape product development and enhancements.

	Prepare regular reports on sales metrics including team performance, revenue growth, and forecasting.

	Maintain a detailed sales pipeline and ensure the accuracy of all sales-related data.

	Manage the sales team budget and ensure resources are allocated effectively to maximize ROI.
	Ensure the sales team has the tools, training, and support necessary to meet objectives.

	Minimum of 3 years of experience, with a proven track record in sales and business development.

	Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with a strategic mindset.

What We Offer


	A dynamic and engaging work environment.

3.)&nbsp;Solar Engineering Associate

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time (Hybrid)
Department: Solar Engineering
Reports To: Solar Engineering Lead

The Role


	Rivy is seeking a highly motivated and technically grounded Junior Solar Engineer to support the design, assessment, implementation, and maintenance of solar energy systems.
	This role is designed for an individual with a strong foundation in renewable energy principles and a keen interest in practical field execution and customer engagement.
	The Junior Solar Engineer will assist in conducting energy audits, performing site assessments, supporting system design and feasibility evaluations, and contributing to project Quality Assurance and Quality Control (QA/QC) processes.
	Working under supervision, the Engineer will also provide technical support to customers, assist in preparing proposals and documentation, and support the monitoring and maintenance of installed asset systems.
	This position offers a structured pathway for professional growth while ensuring high technical standards and effective project delivery across Rivy&rsquo;s solar initiatives.


Job Responsibilities
Customer Engagement &amp; Technical Assessment:


	Assist in customer engagement by understanding their energy needs and requirements under supervision.
	Provide introductory-level advice and recommendations on suitable solar energy solutions.
	Provide basic technical support and guidance to customers.
	Maintain accurate records of customer interactions and technical assessments.
	Support technical assessments of asset and inventory finance loans.


Solar System Design &amp; Energy Audit:


	Conduct energy audits to analyze customer energy consumption patterns and identify optimization opportunities.
	Perform site assessments to evaluate structural suitability, shading, electrical infrastructure, and installation feasibility
	Collect and document site data required for system design and proposal development.
	Aid in evaluating the feasibility and preliminary design of proposed solar installations.
	Assist in system sizing, component selection, and performance estimation under guidance.
	Assist in preparing presentations, proposals and Bills of Quantities (BOQ) for solar projects.
	Support the use of solar design and simulation tools such as Helioscope for system modeling, performance assessment, and proposal development.


Project Quality Assurance &amp; Quality Control (QA/QC):


	Support the implementation of Quality Assurance and Quality Control procedures during project execution.
	Participate in inspection of installations to ensure compliance with design specifications, safety standards, and regulatory requirements.
	Assist in documenting and reporting non-conformities and corrective actions.


Technical Support:


	Support maintenance activities of installed solar PV systems when required.
	Assist in system performance monitoring and troubleshooting.
	&nbsp;Work with vendors to ensure assets are operational.


Learning &amp; Industry Development:


	Stay updated with advancements in the clean energy industry, technologies, and regulations.
	Participate in training and development programs to enhance technical expertise and customer engagement skills.


Qualifications


	Experience: 3+ years of experience in renewable energy, solar engineering, technical project support, or related fields, preferably involving field implementation, system design, or operational monitoring of solar energy projects.
	Technical Literacy: Strong foundational understanding of solar photovoltaic system design, energy auditing, site assessment, and basic principles of solar system performance optimization.
	Industry Awareness: Familiarity with renewable energy technologies, solar project development processes, and quality assurance/quality control standards in solar installations.
	Soft Skills: Excellent analytical thinking, communication, teamwork, and stakeholder engagement abilities.
	Mindset: Demonstrated initiative, problem-solving capability, and a proactive approach to learning and delivering technical and operational objectives.


What We Offer


	A dynamic and engaging&nbsp;&nbsp;work environment.
	Competitive pay and staff benefits.
	Opportunity to work with a passionate and dedicated team.
	Continuous professional development and training opportunities.

Private Banking Officer Job at Abbey Mortgage Bank Plc




        
    

        
        Abbey  Mortgage Bank Plc is the premier, largest and most profitable, non-aligned Primary Mortgage Bank in Nigeria. Abbey is one of the seven national Primary  Mortgage Banks (PMB) licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria and Federal Mortgage Bank of Nigeria (FMBN). The recent recapitalization exercise saw the downsize from 105  mortgage banks to only 34 now surviving. We not only emerged successfully capitalized but one of the few that also met our National obligation as well as amongst the public liability companies.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Private Banking Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Position Type: Full-Time 
     

            
    



Role Overview


	The Private Banking Officer will be responsible for managing the financial needs of high-net-worth individuals, providing tailored banking solutions, investment advisory, and wealth management services while maintaining strong, lasting client relationships.


Key Responsibilities & Competencies


	Deliver personalized banking and financial advisory services to high-net-worth clients in the public sector
	Build, manage, and grow client relationships through tailored investment and wealth management solutions
	Ensure strict compliance with regulatory standards and internal policies
	Manage client portfolios, analyze market trends, and identify new business opportunities
	Drive deposit mobilization and contribute to the bank’s growth strategy.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Business Administration, Economics, or a related field (Master’s degree is an added advantage)
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in private banking or wealth management, preferably with a focus on the public sector
	Strong knowledge of investment products, financial markets, and regulatory frameworks
	Good understanding of compliance requirements, including AML and KYC protocols.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

  "title": "Sales Capability Manager",
  "description": "GBfoods Africa - Our history begins with our brand, Gallina Blanca, founded in Barcelona in 1937.&nbsp; But this is only the beginning of a long and exciting journey.

Today, with turnover of around &euro;1,3 Billion and a staff of around 3,600 employees, GBfoods is present in more than 50 countries in Europe and Africa. They include Spain, Italy, Germany, France, Belgium, the Netherlands, Sweden, Finland, Nigeria, Ghana, Algeria and Senegal, among others.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Capability Manager

Job Location: Nigeria
Department: BU Nigeria
Contract: Permanent

Job Responsibilities
Strategic Capability Leadership:


	Own and drive the end-to-end Sales Capability strategy, aligned to the company&#39;s commercial growth targets and route-to-market model.
	Design competency frameworks and career progression pathways for all sales roles benchmarked against best-in-class FMCG/food manufacturing practice.
	Present capability strategy, budget, and ROI to Commercial Leadership and Executive Committee.
	Own the annual Sales Capability budget, including training vendor contracts, tools, and incentive program spend.


Capability Building &amp; Training:


	Design and deliver advanced training curricula: consultative/solution selling, negotiation, category management, distributor management, and RTM execution.
	Build and lead a team of internal sales trainers/coaches; set their objectives and develop their capability.
	Design and oversee onboarding academies for new sales hires to reduce time-to-productivity.
	Partner with HR/L&amp;D to certify sales staff and integrate capability standards into performance management and promotion criteria.


Field Coaching &amp; Performance:


	Establish a structured field coaching cadence (GROW model or similar) and audit its execution across regions.
	Build the capability of Sales Managers/Supervisors as front-line coaches, holding them accountable for team development.
	Own sales productivity KPIs (volume/value per rep, outlet coverage, perfect store execution, distribution) and drive corrective interventions.


Sales Tools, Process &amp; RTM Design:


	Own the design and continuous improvement of sales playbooks, journey plans, call cycles, and merchandising/execution standards.
	Lead the deployment, adoption, and optimization of SFA/CRM platforms; own data quality and usage governance.
	Design incentive schemes, sales contests, and recognition programs that drive target behaviours, in partnership with Finance and HR.
	Partner with Trade Marketing/Category teams on capability requirements for NPD launches, promotions, and seasonal campaigns.


Distributor &amp; Trade Capability:


	Lead capability-building programs for distributor sales teams, merchandisers, and third-party trade partners.
	Design distributor scorecards and capability audits as part of the broader RTM excellence agenda.


Data, Insight &amp; Continuous Improvement:


	Use sales performance and CRM data to diagnose capability gaps by region/territory/role and prioritise interventions.
	Build dashboards and report that link capability investment to business outcomes (growth, market share, productivity, retention).
	Benchmark capability practices against competitors and global FMCG standards; bring in external best practice.


Requirements


	10&ndash;14+ years of progressive experience in Sales, Trade Marketing, or Sales Capability/L&amp;D roles within FMCG or food manufacturing, including senior field sales leadership (e.g., Regional Sales Manager or above).
	Proven track record designing and scaling sales capability programs across a multi-region sales force.
	Prior management experience leading trainers/coaches or a sales team.
	Deep familiarity with Nigerian RTM structures &mdash; distributors, wholesalers, open markets, modern trade, and informal trade.
	Strategic thinking with the ability to translate commercial strategy into capability and execution plans.
	Strong facilitation, coaching, and executive presentation skills.
	Excellent stakeholder management across Sales, Trade Marketing, HR, Finance, and Distributors.
	Strong people leadership &mdash; able to build, manage, and develop a capability/training team.
	High level of self-motivation, resilience, and willingness to travel extensively.
	Proficiency in SFA/CRM platforms (e.g., Salesforce, SAP, LEAFIO, or similar RTM/DMS platforms) configuration, reporting, and adoption management.
	Advanced Excel (pivot tables, formulas, data modelling) and Power BI or similar BI tools for building performance dashboards.
	Instructional design skills &mdash; able to build training curricula and materials using frameworks such as ADDIE or 70-20-10.
	Working knowledge of Learning Management Systems (LMS) for tracking certification and training completion.
	Data analysis skills &mdash; able to interpret sales/distribution data (sell-in/sell-out, numeric/weighted distribution, share of shelf) to diagnose capability gaps.
	Familiarity with incentive/commission scheme design and modelling.
	Comfortable using PowerPoint/Google Slides for board-level reporting and facilitation materials.

Sales Capability Manager Job at GBfoods Africa




        
    

        
        GBfoods Africa - Our history begins with our brand, Gallina Blanca, founded in Barcelona in 1937.  But this is only the beginning of a long and exciting journey.

Today, with turnover of around €1,3 Billion and a staff of around 3,600 employees, GBfoods is present in more than 50 countries in Europe and Africa. They include Spain, Italy, Germany, France, Belgium, the Netherlands, Sweden, Finland, Nigeria, Ghana, Algeria and Senegal, among others.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Capability Manager

Job Location: Nigeria
Department: BU Nigeria
Contract: Permanent

Job Responsibilities
Strategic Capability Leadership:
     

            
    




	Own and drive the end-to-end Sales Capability strategy, aligned to the company's commercial growth targets and route-to-market model.
	Design competency frameworks and career progression pathways for all sales roles benchmarked against best-in-class FMCG/food manufacturing practice.
	Present capability strategy, budget, and ROI to Commercial Leadership and Executive Committee.
	Own the annual Sales Capability budget, including training vendor contracts, tools, and incentive program spend.


Capability Building & Training:


	Design and deliver advanced training curricula: consultative/solution selling, negotiation, category management, distributor management, and RTM execution.
	Build and lead a team of internal sales trainers/coaches; set their objectives and develop their capability.
	Design and oversee onboarding academies for new sales hires to reduce time-to-productivity.
	Partner with HR/L&D to certify sales staff and integrate capability standards into performance management and promotion criteria.


Field Coaching & Performance:


	Establish a structured field coaching cadence (GROW model or similar) and audit its execution across regions.
	Build the capability of Sales Managers/Supervisors as front-line coaches, holding them accountable for team development.
	Own sales productivity KPIs (volume/value per rep, outlet coverage, perfect store execution, distribution) and drive corrective interventions.


Sales Tools, Process & RTM Design:


	Own the design and continuous improvement of sales playbooks, journey plans, call cycles, and merchandising/execution standards.
	Lead the deployment, adoption, and optimization of SFA/CRM platforms; own data quality and usage governance.
	Design incentive schemes, sales contests, and recognition programs that drive target behaviours, in partnership with Finance and HR.
	Partner with Trade Marketing/Category teams on capability requirements for NPD launches, promotions, and seasonal campaigns.


Distributor & Trade Capability:


	Lead capability-building programs for distributor sales teams, merchandisers, and third-party trade partners.
	Design distributor scorecards and capability audits as part of the broader RTM excellence agenda.


Data, Insight & Continuous Improvement:


	Use sales performance and CRM data to diagnose capability gaps by region/territory/role and prioritise interventions.
	Build dashboards and report that link capability investment to business outcomes (growth, market share, productivity, retention).
	Benchmark capability practices against competitors and global FMCG standards; bring in external best practice.


Requirements


	10–14+ years of progressive experience in Sales, Trade Marketing, or Sales Capability/L&D roles within FMCG or food manufacturing, including senior field sales leadership (e.g., Regional Sales Manager or above).
	Proven track record designing and scaling sales capability programs across a multi-region sales force.
	Prior management experience leading trainers/coaches or a sales team.
	Deep familiarity with Nigerian RTM structures — distributors, wholesalers, open markets, modern trade, and informal trade.
	Strategic thinking with the ability to translate commercial strategy into capability and execution plans.
	Strong facilitation, coaching, and executive presentation skills.
	Excellent stakeholder management across Sales, Trade Marketing, HR, Finance, and Distributors.
	Strong people leadership — able to build, manage, and develop a capability/training team.
	High level of self-motivation, resilience, and willingness to travel extensively.
	Proficiency in SFA/CRM platforms (e.g., Salesforce, SAP, LEAFIO, or similar RTM/DMS platforms) configuration, reporting, and adoption management.
	Advanced Excel (pivot tables, formulas, data modelling) and Power BI or similar BI tools for building performance dashboards.
	Instructional design skills — able to build training curricula and materials using frameworks such as ADDIE or 70-20-10.
	Working knowledge of Learning Management Systems (LMS) for tracking certification and training completion.
	Data analysis skills — able to interpret sales/distribution data (sell-in/sell-out, numeric/weighted distribution, share of shelf) to diagnose capability gaps.
	Familiarity with incentive/commission scheme design and modelling.
	Comfortable using PowerPoint/Google Slides for board-level reporting and facilitation materials.
